reviews.review_msg: Not a hard class, midterm/final was just mcq, the rest of your grade is group dependent and some attendance marks near the end of the sem. Overall, the lectures aren't very useful in terms of learning the actual testable course content, prof is engaging but mostly just yaps about personal stories. Just self test with gpt from slides and textbook.

Prof was very approachable and wanted the best for her students. Group work does take up around 30% of ur grade, but not difficult. Exams are definitely more difficult, but are all mc (very concept based). I'd recommend using quizlet and using chat to make more based on the practice questions given. Lectures are very similar to textbook content.

This professor spends majority of her lecture talking about herself, she will very rarely speak about examinable content… you are better off just studying the text book and praying she doesn't put any bad questions on the exam

I have never questioned the qualifications of a professor until taking this class. Sure she has many degrees which she mentions every class, however she doesn't actually teach or understand how boring her classes are. She talks about herself and life for 70% of the class and only spending about 30% of the time talking about actual course ideas.
Prof wasn't bad but the exams were bad even though they were multiple choice the answers were all the same aside from one word.

Engaging lectures overall. The group report was traumatizing, and exams are tough but fair. Teams are randomly assigned, so your experience can really depend on who you get. There's a 5% attendance bonus. Be aware that TA Ryan is a harsh marker, so exercise caution if he is TAing BUS 381.

Dr. Assadi is definitely caring but the class lectures weren't related enough to the examed content. You could read the chapters with attending, take the all MCQ exam and be just fine. I think she could lecture more on what's being tested as questions in the exam were only covered in the textbook not lectures. Overall she's super nice, I would rec.
Terrible professor. Educated at some scam online university and it shows. Might as well just watch YouTube videos about HR. Nonsense course

She was pretty bad. her exams dont even test on proper concepts for half of them. she tests on small things that you wouldn't think are important so don't recommend. she also doesn't know how to spell on tests which can lead to confusion. not a great teacher at all

All MC midterm, final, one group paper and 5% bonus attendance mark. Memorize everything on her slides & textbook with an emphasis on slides (e.g person and union's names, graphs and concepts). She's passionate about HR and teaching, and her lectures are gems filled with industry stories, career advice, and real-world insight.
One of the worst professors at Beedie. She doesn't know what she's talking about and puts little effort into creating effective evaluations. Just bad.

Professor Assadi gives incredible lectures, she is funny, but doesn't veer off topic much. Go to the lectures, there are bonus points that go toward your final. Speaking of her exams, they are incredibly disorganized and confused weirdly. Plus, her group assignments SUCK. She's amazing, but the course structure/exams needs serious revamping.

One of the best lecturers I've had. 1 big group project. Textbook not needed but helps for the exams. Participation is bonus points so not required to attend. She doesn't really veer off topic so much as gives complimentary real world advice. Speaking as an older student who's been in the workforce for 15 years...listen to her: It's good advice.

Prof is horrible. To do well, memorize every detail on her slides & show up to 5 classes (up to 5 bonus marks so people don't skip). Her instructions & grading criteria are unclear and unless asked she will forget. There is a midterm and final (all MC's) and one group term paper. Lectures are boring and she spends the majority of class off topic.
Midterm was pretty hard, lots of tricky questions.

Dr. Assadi had decent lectures. At times, it got boring but she'd use videos and class participation to make it more engaging. Make sure you study well for the exams as a majority of the grades are on there and its fairly easy to study for if you put effort using flashcards and memorization and really understanding the concepts.

Good teacher. As long as you read and understand textbook/lectures you are going to do great! The group projects are kinda annoying but you just gotta deal with it. Other than that it was an okay class, lectures would just get boring.

Super nice prof! Class was based on 2 group projects, tutorial attendance, a midterm and a final (non cumulative). Memorize everything from the slides including examples, and understand the concepts to be able to apply in scenario based questions. Textbook is a lot of reading, majority of the content is already on the slides.
At times this class was extremely easy and at times I wanted to drive off a cliff because of it.

I don't know how people are complaining are so much that final was very easy if you just read all the lectures even like one time. Don't get thrown off by negative reviews, prof is completely fine, it's just the actual content is so boring.
Just avoid at all costs

The midterms and finals are absolutely brutal including random excerpts from examples in lecture as questions. Papers are graded brutally and the TA didn't seem to actually put in much effort. Prof seems superficially nice but I cannot say one good thing about this course.
Everyone, please choose Prof.Assadi's 272/381 class please! I took both with her, she is really a great one for all of the HR faculty!!!!
Best prof ever for all the BUS272 Instructors!
Super kind and helpful Prof! Just choose her without any doubts!
She is so nice! When I talked to her about the concerns about the exams, she comforted me and really gave a lot of useful tips to write and review the exams!!! Thank you Bahareh!
This lady is the best prof for BUS272, with not too many team works and an easier workload for the course! As a lower-division student, I highly recommend.
Really nice Prof, compare to the E100 section, she is the GOAT!!
Awful Professor, told by her that "as long as I did the peer view, you will receive full mark". But by the end, she didn't follow her word. Hope she can one day be disqualified by the SFU. Ewwww

Midterms and finals have 3 versions. One is easy while two are extremely difficult, hence resulting in a high curve. If you get a bad group not much will be done. The ones with the harder paper will always get a bad grade. Please just avoid if you want a good grade.

Bahareh was a great professor! If you want to do well in her course remember to assist to the tutorials! Also, if you participate in her class I think it will count as your participation grade and it will add up so much towards your grade! Although participation is not mandatory during her class, it is strongly encouraged!

there's so many reviews saying that she's not a good prof, so I was worried when I took this course with her, but she's a great prof I think people are just lazy and don't study and then blame it on the prof :/. She gives extra credit each class if you participate, her lectures are also really interesting and she always ends class early

Dr. Assadi is a wonderful professor. She cares about her students and gives lots of opportunities for extra credit. She is very friendly and makes her lectures a very welcoming space for students to participate. She is hard marker but if you read the textbook and really understand the information you will do just fine in her class.

i was really nervous to take bus381 with her after reading all her low reviews, but after taking her class, i can vouch that she is a great lecturer who genuinely cares about HR and your learning. super approachable, kind, and helpful. my one criticism is her participation bonus marks make lectures longer than needed and can side track lecture.
She simply reads off her slides, not much value offered from her lectures. Get ready to memorize all the tiniest details from the textbook for exams.
I lvoe this lady

Do not take this prof. Her midterm and final are MC straight from the textbook. Her lectures are all definitions from the textbook. How she taught the course gave no value to business decision making. The course is so dry and all theory.

The most poorly designed course I have ever taken (BUS 381). Assadi was very lazy, just read off lecture slides every class. Didn't want to do any marking so exams were all multiple choice (40 question midterm, 80 question final), didn't encourage actual understanding of the concepts, and made it very difficult to do well. She really doesn't care..

Dr.Assadi is a good lecturer as she has lots of HR related experiences to tie into the lectures. Your grade is made up mainly with midterm, final, group paper. The group papers are marked really hard and wasn't fun to do. Final was really difficult because the questions seem to expect you to memorize the entire textbook and each little detail.

In terms of lectures, she isn't the driest or worst prof I've had, but in terms of marking, she's brutal. Exam questions are usually multiple choice but too vague to have one clear answer, and you won't be able to argue your reasoning to her because she is very stubborn. Study the textbook completely, as that's where most exam questions are from.

Long lectures, but she was one of the best lecturers I've had at SFU. Clearly has a lot of real life experience in Human Resources. Well spoken with clear examples. Graded only on 2 projects (1 group 1 solo), a midterm and a final. Graded fairly hard, but got a B overall with a pretty average effort.

Some students argue that Assadi was bland and boring, but I argue that the course, in general, is blonde. I managed to get a A, from just last-minute cramming and didn't mind Assadi at all. Every chapter you finish every week, just summarize it in your own words and you will def get a good mark.

As an intro course, expect to memorize a ton of information with little direction for midterms and finals. I really did not enjoy this class, I found the concepts boring and the teaching style of Dr Assadi ineffective as there wasn't much room for active learning in an engaging way. Shes a bit harsh and cold so beware
This class completely disinterested me from the topic of HR. The 3 hour lectures were dry and boring, with little room for engagement. Questions on the exam were ridiculous. Would not take again.

The moment she spoke I knew right away that shes good. I could listen to her speak for 2 hrs & I learned a lot about OB. To be fair, she gave us full marks for questions in Finals that were not taught in class & we got 80 mins for 70 questions. Yes she has high standards & I still don't have the courage to read her feedback on our group paper :)

Most unfair teacher I have ever had and I've had many. Do not take any course with her. The test was crazy. As a student that does not reform well under pressure this class messed me up. The ta was really nice tho. The final was and the quizzes are 1 minute per question. Which is impossible. Question type was comprehensive with one min per quest...
She's a good prof. There is a group paper and presentation, plus weekly quizzes.

Bahareh is a good prof. She teaches well and explains all course concepts well. However, she is such a tough marker that it's hard to get a good grade in this class and the final exam will drop your mark because she may put questions in there you didn't really learn about. Overall she is nice and cares about her students but be aware of her marking

Shes a good prof! Engaging lectures and weekly quizzes are easy. However, I was stuck with a very uncooperative group for the project and she was not at all very helpful with the situation. Often profs dont change groups so be prepared to work in a major project with people who possibly may not do any work and a prof who wont do much about it

She is a very calm and caring prof. Her lectures were interesting and the assignments were not too difficult. The assignments made up the majority of the grade, with one final exam at the end of the semester. Overall, would highly recommend for any HR courses.

Lectures were engaging, although it seemed like a reiteration of the textbook, she presented it well with her own additions. Grading criteria for everything was very vague so it was hard to do well. Group paper was also a pain to do. Final was very hard and didn't make much sense. Time limit was too short since all questions were scenarios.

I took this prof cuz of the good reviews. She is a nice and calm prof but her final exam was not as what we expected. She clearly made the TAs to create quiz and exam questions. Final was all scenario questions with a short time limit. I am pretty sure she mentioned that it was going to be open book but changed it to close book last min!

Her lectures were clear and participation helped us learn and apply the material. The tests were mostly based on lectures. Group paper is tough as you cant pick group members and there is no way to report how much they contributed. Class average was high

Bahareh Assadi was a great lecturer. She never had long lectures and made sure to stay engaging throughout them. There was a multiple choice only midterm and final, as well as a group paper. Everything seemed very fair and her feedback on the paper was very valuable. I wish more profs were like her.

Assadi is do doubt the best prof out there. She is really motivated, and inspirational. She made the class so much fun, by sharing stories from her own life. The class is overall a gpa booster. Really helpful and gives a lot of feedback. Exams are based on slides and super easy.
Bahareh is very knowledgeable and has high interest in her field, always making the lectures interesting and engaging. Very good Instructor and very caring.
She is lovely and knowledgeable. Her lecture slides are very informational. The class curve is high. Recommended.

Overall, shes a pretty sweet and caring prof. Very easy to talk to and very approachable. But be warned, the average is high and this course is group intensive so start praying for a randomized group selection throughout your semester. Mid term and Final is all just off her lecture slides.

Great prof. Before taking her course, I had 0 interest in human resources. Lectures are engaging, and workload is pretty minimal. Marking is fair, but not lenient. Attendance isnt mandatory, but her exams are based on them pretty heavily. She did seem to be a little hurt that a lot of people stopped attending regularly last few weeks of term, haha.
